commitments.text: ongoing work with the United States on a continental approach to addressing methane emissions reductions in the oil and gas sectors

measurability.reason: The commitment lacks a specific target year, baseline year, and target value.

gap_findings.description: Canada's NDC does not specify a methane reduction target, whereas IPCC AR6 recommends reducing methane ~34% by 2030 vs 2020.
gap_findings.ipcc_evidence: IPCC AR6 WG3 SPM: reducing methane ~34% by 2030 vs 2020 buys near-term warming relief and is cost-effective.

gap_findings.description: Canada's NDC commitment is conditional on international finance and lacks quantification, baseline, or timeline.

gap_findings.ipcc_evidence: IPCC AR6 WG3 SPM: reducing methane ~34% by 2030 vs 2020 buys near-term warming relief and is cost-effective.

gap_findings.description: Canada's NDC commitment is conditional on international finance, which creates delivery risk.

pass_c.country_summary: Canada's NDC commitments on methane reduction are vague and lack quantification, while IPCC AR6 recommends a specific 34% reduction by 2030.
pass_c.policy_recommendation: Canada should specify a clear, quantifiable methane reduction target and ensure it is unconditional to meet the IPCC's recommended 34% reduction by 2030.
